title: S3 Objects, Keys, Metadata, Tags, Listing, and Consistency · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for s3 objects, keys, metadata, tags, listing, and consistency and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for S3 object-model and listing invariant table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish bucket, key, version ID, delete marker, upload ID, part number, and application identity.
Design opaque or structured keys with explicit delimiter, normalization, tenant, cardinality, migration, and disclosure rules.
Choose system metadata, user metadata, object tags, database metadata, and event payloads by mutability, limits, query, trust, and lifecycle needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Bucket Key Version Identity · changed case · Quiz Bucket Key Version Identity
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 9762 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate object key, version, pagination, and strong-read evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish bucket, key, version ID, delete marker, upload ID, part number, and application identity.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go List Pagination Order · changed case · Quiz List Pagination Order
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 1667 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate object key, version, pagination, and strong-read evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Paginate ListObjectsV2 with continuation tokens, delimiter and prefix rules, duplicate-safe consumers, and no universal ordering assumption.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use S3 strong read-after-write consistency while preserving single-key atomicity and cross-object transaction limits.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use S3 strong read-after-write consistency while preserving single-key atomicity and cross-object transaction limits.
